headline: The educators converge on a single argument this cycle: the harness around a coding agent, its context, memory, evaluation, and logs, matters more than the model itself.
body: Cole Medin's reading of Google's agentic engineering playbook anchors the cycle, arguing the model accounts for only 10% of results while context engineering, tools, and verification systems drive the rest, and tracing the shift from vibe coding to agentic orchestration with its token economics for 2026 [0]. The AI Engineer talks reinforce this from the architecture side: Victor Savkin frames current agents as a genius with amnesia that lacks persistent memory [5], Ishaan Sehgal proposes anchoring agent identity in durable append-only session logs as models commoditize [7], and Angie Jones argues coding agents raise engineering to the system level rather than eliminating it [13]. Production concerns recur in OpenGov's deployment of OG Assist with A2A and long-context handling [4] and Meta's continuous online evaluation pipelines for detecting drift and hallucinations [12], while tooling lands in open agentic coding models like Ornith [2], note-to-memory pipelines [1], and Google Cloud's data agents exposed through ADK and MCP [11].

cited_items.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
